result:
Connection pool = reusable set of open DB connections.

Why: opening TCP+auth per query slow (10-100ms). Pool keeps N connections warm, hands one to each request, returns on release.

Knobs:
- min/max size — floor/ceiling of open conns
- idle timeout — close conn after X sec unused
- max lifetime — recycle conn after Y sec (avoid stale)
- acquire timeout — fail fast if pool exhausted

Flow: app asks pool → pool gives free conn or waits → app runs query → app returns conn → pool keeps or closes.

Gotchas:
- pool size > DB max_connections = errors
- long-held transactions starve pool
- per-process pool, not per-thread — multiply by worker count
- serverless: pool dies with process, use external pooler (pgbouncer)

Common libs: HikariCP (Java), pgbouncer (Postgres proxy), SQLAlchemy pool (Python), pg-pool (Node).
